message:
feat(db): ATC v17 schema family migrated via canonical-only follow-up runner

Lands the schema surface and migration plumbing for ATC tracks
`br-bn0vb.22/.24/.27/.28`. The ATC tables and indexes are now owned
by the canonical-only runtime follow-up runner instead of the base
FrankenConnection-safe runner, because ATC writes already bypass
FrankenConnection after repeated corruption reports and need to hit
the canonical SQLite path end-to-end.

Schema changes (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs`)
---------------------------------------------------------
- New `is_atc_runtime_canonical_migration(id)` predicate enumerates
  the full ATC schema family that must run on canonical SQLite only:
  `v16_create_atc_experiences`, `v16a_atc_experiences_*` column
  adds, `v16_create_atc_experience_rollups`,
  `v16_idx_atc_experiences_*`, `v16_analyze_atc_experiences`,
  `v16b_atc_rollups_add_*`,
  `v17_idx_atc_experiences_decision_effect_unique`,
  `v17_create_atc_leader_lease`,
  `v17_atc_experiences_add_contained_suspected_secret`,
  `v17_atc_experiences_add_privacy_classification`,
  `v17_create_atc_rollup_snapshots`,
  `v17_idx_atc_rollup_snapshots_captured`,
  `v18_rollup_*`, and
  `v21_atc_experiences_add_feature_schema_version`.
- `is_unsupported_by_franken` now returns `true` for everything that
  `is_atc_runtime_canonical_migration` picks up, so the base-mode
  runner skips the entire ATC family.
- `schema_migrations_runtime_canonical_followup` no longer
  hand-lists the case-insensitive agent index + recipients_json
  chain; it filters by the shared
  `is_runtime_canonical_followup_migration` predicate so every
  follow-up migration (ATC + the existing
  `v10a_dedup_agents_case_insensitive`, `v10b_idx_agents_project_
  name_nocase`, `v15_add_recipients_json_to_messages`,
  `v15b_backfill_recipients_json`,
  `v15c_trg_messages_default_recipients_json`) flows through a
  single gate.
- Schema-listing regression tests are flipped: the base-mode
  assertions now say `!ids.contains("v16_create_atc_experiences")`,
  `!ids.contains("v17_create_atc_leader_lease")`, and the matching
  negations for every ATC ID, because base mode must leave that
  surface to canonical-only.

Pool init (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs`)
--------------------------------------------------
`run_sqlite_init_once` now captures the follow-up runner outcome so
the init log can report how many canonical-only migrations actually
applied. Doc comment rewritten: the follow-up phase owns the
remaining runtime-only schema family, not just recipients_json /
agent-index, reflecting the expanded ATC responsibilities.

Library re-exports (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/lib.rs`)
---------------------------------------------------------
`ExpiredExperienceCandidate` joins the existing `OpenExperienceFilter`,
`OpenExperienceSummary`, `RollupEntry` re-exports from
`atc_queries`; relocated next to the other ATC exports for
discoverability.

Queries (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs`)
---------------------------------------------------
+416 lines implementing the ATC CRUD that the new schema backs —
open-experience fetch, leader-lease acquire/renew/release, rollup
append, privacy-classification enforcement, and the
`ExpiredExperienceCandidate` reaper query — all operating against
`DbPool` with consistent `cx`-aware outcomes. Behaviour-level
coverage for this API lives in the per-crate lifecycle and
leader-lease tests updated in this commit.

Test scaffolding tidy-up
------------------------
`tests/atc_experience_lifecycle.rs` and `tests/atc_leader_lease.rs`
pick up the import reshuffle that came with the `lib.rs` re-export
move, plus cargo-fmt-driven line-break tweaks in the resolution
branches (e.g. `ResolutionKind::Censored { ts_micros: ... }` split
across multiple lines, the longer `assert_eq!` messages wrapped).
No test behaviour changes.

Search-service cosmetics (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/search_service.rs`)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
cargo-fmt split a handful of long row-extraction chains
(`row.get(N).and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64).unwrap_or(0)`)
across multiple lines and wrapped the `ack_required`/`created_ts`
`Some(...)` constructions. No logic change.

Migration doc (`docs/MIGRATION_v16_to_v17.md`)
----------------------------------------------
New operator-facing note enumerating the v17 additive migrations
(the lease table, two `atc_experiences` columns, the rollup
snapshots table + capture-time index), confirming no down-migration
exists, and pinning the scope boundary: this patch owns schema +
migration coverage only. Runtime producer logic for the new privacy
fields stays with `br-bn0vb.22`, leader-election behaviour on top of
`atc_leader_lease` stays with `br-bn0vb.24`, and snapshot
serialization stays with `br-bn0vb.27`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

             .into_iter()
             .map(|row| SearchResult {
                 doc_kind,
-                id: row.get(0).and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64).unwrap_or(0),
+                id: row
+                    .get(0)
+                    .and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64)
+                    .unwrap_or(0),
                 title: row.get_as::<String>(1).unwrap_or_default(),
                 importance: Some(row.get_as::<String>(2).unwrap_or_default()),
-                ack_required: Some(row.get(3).and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64).unwrap_or(0) != 0),
-                created_ts: Some(row.get(4).and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64).unwrap_or(0)),
+                ack_required: Some(
+                    row.get(3)
+                        .and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64)
+                        .unwrap_or(0)
+                        != 0,
+                ),
+                created_ts: Some(
+                    row.get(4)
+                        .and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64)
+                        .unwrap_or(0),
+                ),
                 thread_id: row.get_as::<Option<String>>(5).unwrap_or_default(),
                 from_agent: Some(
                     row.get_as::<String>(6)
@@ -3863,7 +3875,11 @@ fn map_planned_rows(rows: Vec<sqlmodel_core::Row>, doc_kind: DocKind) -> Vec<Sea
                 .or_else(|| Some(UNKNOWN_SENDER_DISPLAY.to_string())),
                 from_agent_id: row.get(7).and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64),
                 body: row.get_as::<String>(8).unwrap_or_default(),
-                project_id: Some(row.get(9).and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64).unwrap_or(0)),
+                project_id: Some(
+                    row.get(9)
+                        .and_then(crate::queries::value_as_i64)
+                        .unwrap_or(0),
+                ),
                 score: Some(row.get_as::<f64>(10).unwrap_or(0.0)),
                 ..SearchResult::default()
             })

@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+# ATC Migration: v16 -> v17 Surface
+
+Tracks the additive ATC schema surface introduced for `br-bn0vb.28`.
+
+## What Changed
+
+The ATC migration set now reserves schema for the next ATC seams:
+
+- `atc_leader_lease` table for DB-backed leader election state
+- `atc_experiences.contained_suspected_secret` with default `0`
+- `atc_experiences.privacy_classification` with default `legacy_unclassified`
+- `atc_rollup_snapshots` table plus capture-time index
+
+These migrations are implemented inline in
+[`crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs`](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs)
+because this repo does not use per-version migration files.
+
+## Migration IDs
+
+The new additive migrations are:
+
+- `v17_create_atc_leader_lease`
+- `v17_atc_experiences_add_contained_suspected_secret`
+- `v17_atc_experiences_add_privacy_classification`
+- `v17_create_atc_rollup_snapshots`
+- `v17_idx_atc_rollup_snapshots_captured`
+
+## Operator Notes
+
+- The migration is additive and idempotent. No tables or columns are dropped.
+- Existing `atc_experiences` rows receive:
+  - `contained_suspected_secret = 0`
+  - `privacy_classification = 'legacy_unclassified'`
+- There is no down-migration. Downgrading would require manual destructive cleanup
+  of the new ATC tables/columns and is intentionally unsupported.
+
+## Verification
+
+The migration/test coverage for this surface lives in:
+
+- [`crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/schema_migration.rs`](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/tests/schema_migration.rs)
+- [`crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs`](/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs)
+
+Verified lanes:
+
+- fresh DB -> latest schema includes the ATC v17 surface
+- pre-v17 seeded ATC row -> latest schema preserves the row and backfills defaults
+- invalid `privacy_classification` values fail the DB constraint
+- `reconstruct_from_archive` recreates the v17 schema surface
+
+## Important Scope Boundary
+
+This patch lands the schema surface and migration coverage only.
+
+- `br-bn0vb.24` still owns leader-election behavior on top of `atc_leader_lease`
+- `br-bn0vb.27` still owns snapshot serialization/restore behavior on top of `atc_rollup_snapshots`
+- `br-bn0vb.22` owns the runtime producer logic that will populate the new privacy fields
